message:
fix(dep): correct Mermaid graph direction and use println for clean output

Fix two issues in the Mermaid diagram output for dependency trees:

1. Use println! directly instead of ctx.print() to avoid rich_rust markup
   interpretation that was corrupting the Mermaid syntax output.

2. Correct the edge direction in the graph. Previously showed:
   `child --> parent` which implied child depends on parent.
   Now correctly shows: `parent --> child` indicating the dependency
   flows from the blocking issue (parent) to the blocked issue (child).

The Mermaid output now renders correctly in documentation tools and
accurately represents the dependency graph structure where arrows
point from blockers to the issues they block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
